title: fimbristylis autumnalis var. mucronulata
intro: Fimbristylis autumnalis var. mucronulata, commonly known as the autumnal fimbristylis, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the cpermg family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The therapeutic actions of Fimbristylis autumnalis var. mucronulata include its ability to modulate the immune system, exhibit antimicrobial properties, and demonstrate antiviral activity. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties and may provide protection against oxidative stress and inflammation.

parts: Fimbristylis autumnalis var. mucronulata has many medicinal parts, such as the rhizomes, roots, and leaves. The rhizomes of Fimbristylis autumnalis var. mucronulata contain sesquiterpenoids, including furanosesquiterpenes, while the roots contain alkaloids, including quinoline alkaloids. The leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, and also contain phenolic acids.
